identity.name: Lesson
identity.definition: A Lesson is a structured, interactive instructional content unit from Nearpod containing slides, activities, and assessments that a teacher can deliver live or assign self-paced.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Lesson appears in the library browser and search results

attributes.name: Lesson Title
attributes.description: Primary identifier displayed in library and during delivery.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Subject area: science, ela, math, social-studies.

attributes.name: Lesson Type
attributes.description: Delivery mode: live, self-paced, or both.

attributes.name: Slide Count
attributes.description: Total number of slides including content slides and activities.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Lesson appears in tables, search results, and management views

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state: draft, published, archived.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: Full Lesson view in the editor or preview mode

attributes.name: Usage Count
attributes.description: Number of times the lesson has been delivered.

stories.title: Browse and Launch Lesson
stories.benefit: I can find standards-aligned content and deliver it live to my class immediately.
stories.whenClause: I search the Nearpod library and select a lesson,
stories.thenClause: I can preview its slides and activities, then launch it as a live session with a single click.

stories.title: Create Custom Lesson
stories.benefit: I can build interactive content tailored to my students' specific needs and curriculum.
stories.whenClause: I create a new lesson from scratch,
stories.thenClause: I can add slides, embed activities, set scoring, and publish it to my library for repeated use.

stories.title: Join and Complete Lesson
stories.benefit: I can participate in interactive activities and demonstrate my understanding.
stories.whenClause: I join a lesson using the session code,
stories.thenClause: I see the teacher's slides, respond to activities in real time, and receive immediate feedback on scored items.

businessRules.title: Minimum Content
businessRules.description: A Lesson must contain at least one slide to be published. An empty Lesson remains in draft.

businessRules.title: Subject and Grade Required
businessRules.description: Subject and Grade Band are required fields. They drive library filtering and standards alignment.

businessRules.title: Lesson Type Determines Delivery
businessRules.description: A 'live' lesson can only be launched as a Live Session. A 'self-paced' lesson can only be assigned. A 'both' lesson supports either delivery mode.

businessRules.title: Publishing Rules
businessRules.description: Publishing a Lesson locks its content for delivery. To modify a published Lesson, the teacher must create a new version or revert to draft.

businessRules.title: Duplication
businessRules.description: Duplicating a Lesson copies all slides, activities, and configuration. Usage metrics, scores, and reports are not copied.

businessRules.title: Sharing Scope
businessRules.description: A teacher can share Lessons with other teachers in their school. District-wide sharing requires admin approval.

businessRules.title: Library Source Tracking
businessRules.description: Lessons from the Nearpod Library are tagged with source 'nearpod-library'. Teacher-created lessons are 'teacher-created'. Shared lessons from colleagues are 'shared'.

businessRules.title: Archival
businessRules.description: Archived lessons are hidden from the active library but their historical reports and scores remain accessible.

businessRules.title: Slide Count Calculation
businessRules.description: Slide Count includes both content slides and activity slides. It is recalculated whenever slides are added or removed.

businessRules.title: Duration Estimation
businessRules.description: Duration is estimated by the teacher or calculated from Nearpod library metadata. It is advisory and not enforced during delivery.

businessRules.title: Standards Alignment
businessRules.description: Skills/Standards references are optional but recommended. They power standards-based filtering in the library and reporting.

states.name: Draft
states.description: Lesson is being authored or edited
states.triggers: Created by teacher or reverted from published

states.name: Published
states.description: Lesson is finalized and available for delivery

states.name: Archived
states.description: Lesson is retired from active use but data retained
states.triggers: Teacher or admin archives the lesson

relationships.mechanics: Activities are created within a Lesson using the lesson editor. The teacher selects an activity type and configures it inline.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Lesson contains zero or more Activities; each Activity belongs to exactly one Lesson.
relationships.sorts: By position within lesson (default), by activity type.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by scoring method.
relationships.dependencies: Deleting a Lesson deletes all contained Activities. Duplicating a Lesson duplicates all Activities with reset metrics.

relationships.mechanics: A Live Session is created when a teacher launches a Lesson for live delivery. The Session references the source Lesson.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Lesson can be launched as many Live Sessions over time; each Live Session references exactly one Lesson.
relationships.sorts: By start time (most recent first), by class.
relationships.filters: By session status (active/ended), by class, by date range.
relationships.dependencies: A Live Session requires its source Lesson to exist. If the Lesson is archived, existing session data persists but new sessions cannot be launched.

relationships.mechanics: Reports are auto-generated after a Lesson delivery (live session or self-paced assignment) completes.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. Each delivery of a Lesson produces one Report; a Report references exactly one Lesson.
relationships.sorts: By generated date (most recent first), by report type.
relationships.filters: By report type (live/self-paced/combined), by class, by date range.
relationships.dependencies: Reports persist even if the source Lesson is archived or deleted.

nestedObjects.name: Activity
nestedObjects.description: Interactive elements embedded within the lesson

nestedObjects.name: Live Session
nestedObjects.description: Real-time delivery instances of this lesson

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Student performance data from lesson deliveries

allAttributes.name: Lesson Title
allAttributes.description: Primary identifier displayed in library and during delivery.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Subject area. Values: science, ela, math, social-studies.

allAttributes.name: Skills/Standards
allAttributes.description: Aligned skills and standards for filtering and reporting.

allAttributes.name: Lesson Type
allAttributes.description: Delivery mode. Values: live, self-paced, both.

allAttributes.name: Slide Count
allAttributes.description: Total number of slides including content and activity slides.

allAttributes.name: Author
allAttributes.description: Creator of the lesson: teacher name or 'Nearpod Content Team'.

allAttributes.name: Source
allAttributes.description: Origin of the lesson. Values: nearpod-library, teacher-created, shared.

allAttributes.name: Thumbnail
allAttributes.description: Cover image URL for library display.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Lifecycle state. Values: draft, published, archived.

allAttributes.name: Usage Count
allAttributes.description: Number of times the lesson has been delivered (live or self-paced).

allAttributes.name: Average Score
allAttributes.description: Mean score percentage across all deliveries.

allAttributes.name: Created Date
allAttributes.description: When the lesson was first created.

allAttributes.name: Last Used Date
allAttributes.description: When the lesson was most recently delivered.

structure.evidence: A Lesson has extensive attributes: title, subject, grade band, type, slide count, duration, author, source, status, standards alignment, and usage metrics. It fully supports a detail page.

instances.evidence: 'Introduction to Ecosystems' (science, 3-5, live, 18 slides), 'Fractions: Adding & Subtracting' (math, 3-5, self-paced, 24 slides), 'Civil Rights Movement' (social-studies, 6-8, both, 30 slides). Nearpod's library contains thousands of lessons.

purpose.evidence: Lessons are the primary content container in Nearpod. Teachers browse, create, and deliver them. Students join and complete them. Reports aggregate their outcomes. Every Nearpod workflow starts with a Lesson.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object and the central content unit of the Nearpod ecosystem. All delivery and reporting flows through Lessons.

synonyms.notes: Used in data models and API endpoints
Used when lessons are grouped into larger units
Reflects the visual similarity to presentation software

objectViews.description: Lessons shown as cards with thumbnails, subject, grade band, and duration. Teachers browse the library to find lessons to preview, launch live, or save as favorites.
The full lesson authoring view — slides, activities, standards alignment, and editing controls. Authors build and refine lesson content from this page.
The lesson displayed as a minimal header during live delivery — just the title and slide count, staying out of the way of the content.
Lessons listed with usage counts, average scores, and status. Teachers review which lessons landed well and which need revision.
